Sport|Olympics

The 2024Olympic Gamesare upon us, withathleticsno doubt poised once again to provide some of the biggest highlights of all in Paris.

The track action is always a vital part of the world’s biggest sporting event and this summer is certainly no exception as scores of global stars seek to live out their Olympic dreams at an iconic venue at the Stade de France.

The delayed Tokyo Games in 2021 provided some unforgettable moments in athletics, not leastLamont Marcell Jacobs’ shock men’s 100m goldfor Italy.

Jacobs is out to defend his title in Paris, though Jamaica’s Elaine Thompson-Herah will not repeat her 100m and 200m women’s double from Tokyo as the result of an Achilles injury.

Kishane Thompson,Noah Lylesand Oblique Seville are among the favourites to dethrone Jacobs, while Sha'Carri Richardson and Shericka Jackson will surely duel again in another thrilling USA vs Jamaica sprint showdown.

There are plenty of British medal hopes on the track after anencouraging showing at the recent London Diamond League meeting, with Keely Hodgkinson gunning for women’s 800m gold in the absence of rival and defending champion Athing Mu, who fell during the US Olympic trials and did not make the American squad for Paris.

Matthew Hudson-Smith is also the favourite for the men’s 400m title after breaking his own European record, with the likes of Dina Asher-Smith, Josh Kerr, Laura Muir and Katarina Johnson-Thompson among the others in Britain’s 64-strong track and field team hoping to finish on the podium in Paris.

Here is the full 2024 Olympics track and field schedule (all times BST)...

title: Paris Olympics 2024 athletics schedule in full: Events, UK start times and dates | The Standard
author: George Flood
description: Potentially epic 100m and 200m battles headline track and field action at the Stade de France
keywords: Paris 2024, Paris Olympics, Olympic Games, noah lyles
